diagrams.title: Symbolic AI vs Data-Driven AI: Side-by-Side Comparison
diagrams.description: A split diagram showing two parallel pathways - left side depicts Symbolic AI with knowledge base and rules processing, right side shows Data-Driven AI with neural network and training data. Arrows show the flow from input to output for each approach.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the fundamental difference: Symbolic AI uses explicit rules and logic to reason, while Data-Driven AI learns patterns from large amounts of data through neural networks

diagrams.title: AI Approach Spectrum: From Rules to Data
diagrams.description: A horizontal spectrum diagram showing where different AI methodologies fall between pure Symbolic (left) and pure Data-Driven (right). Includes positioned markers for Expert Systems, Rule-based ML, Neuro-symbolic AI, Deep Learning, and foundation models.
elements: Gradient coloring from rule-focused to data-focused
Labels: Expert Systems, Rule-based ML, Neuro-symbolic, Deep Learning
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates that AI approaches exist on a continuum rather than being mutually exclusive, and shows the evolution from pure rule-based systems to pure learning-based systems

diagrams.title: Processing Comparison: Knowledge Engineering vs Feature Learning
diagrams.description: Two vertical flow diagrams comparing how each approach processes information. Left shows Symbolic pipeline: Domain Expert → Knowledge Engineering → Explicit Rules → Reasoning. Right shows Data-Driven pipeline: Raw Data → Feature Engineering → Model Training → Prediction.
diagrams.purpose: Highlights the key distinction in how each approach acquires capability - Symbolic AI requires human experts to encode knowledge explicitly, while Data-Driven AI automatically learns features and patterns from raw data
